cards.title: I wish my son wanted to spend more time with me
cards.body: A parent struggles with their adult son's limited contact despite their devotion. A therapist explores whether the parent might be unintentionally pressuring their child.

cards.title: Having children makes you smarter? Incredible, but true
cards.body: Recent studies show mothers and fathers have healthier, younger-looking brains than non-parents. Years of mental and emotional gymnastics with children may boost cognitive health.

cards.title: I kept fearing the worst on family holiday
cards.body: A parent learns to manage anxiety during family time. When a real emergency occurs, they discover unexpected resilience.

cards.title: Oliver's mum was a narcissist and his dad avoidant
cards.body: Dysfunctional childhood patterns can persist into adulthood relationships. Understanding healthy relationship dynamics requires time and conscious effort to break inherited patterns.

cards.title: I always take my Dad's advice – do the opposite
cards.body: A child reflects on when parental advice stops fitting their own identity. Learning to trust yourself over authority figures is a key developmental milestone.

cards.title: My father left my mother for another woman
cards.body: A parent navigates family dynamics after divorce and new relationships. Children must balance fairness with what maintains important relationships.

cards.title: Raising Emotionally Healthy Kids in the Digital Age
cards.body: Veteran educators share practical strategies for supporting children's emotional development online. Healthy parenting in the digital age requires intentional attention and boundaries.

cards.title: Psychopathy in Children: What Can Be Done?
cards.body: Early identification of psychopathic traits and callous-unemotional behavior in children enables timely intervention. Schools and families play crucial roles in treatment and support.

cards.title: The Power of Developing Executive Function
cards.body: Executive function skills are foundational for children's success and well-being. These abilities shape how kids manage time, emotions, and problem-solving.

cards.title: When Scrolling Becomes a Problem
cards.body: Growing evidence documents serious health risks from problematic social media use in youth. Parents and guardians need awareness of warning signs and intervention strategies.

cards.title: Why We Should Protect Childlike Imagination
cards.body: Imagination is a vital capacity that children naturally possess but can lose. Nurturing creative thinking supports healthy child development and lifelong resilience.

cards.title: Why Your Body Freezes, Flees, or Fights Under Stress
cards.body: Children's stress responses are physiological and not inherent character flaws. Understanding fight-flight-freeze reactions helps parents respond with compassion.
